engineIntelligence.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Skoda Kodiaq in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Skoda Kodiaq DFGA engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR cooler leak - coolant enters cylinders causing white smoke and misfires on high-mileage units.
DPF clogging - frequent short journeys cause ash buildup, leading to restricted exhaust flow.

cards.altText: Skoda Kodiaq DADA engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - early EA211 evo units experience chain elongation causing rattling on cold starts.
Carbon buildup - direct injection leads to intake valve carbon deposits affecting idle quality.

cards.altText: Skoda Kodiaq DKZA engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - hydraulic tensioner fails causing chain slap and potential engine interference.
Water pump leak - plastic impeller and housing degrade, causing coolant loss and overheating.

commonProblems.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Skoda Kodiaq is generally robust, but its EA288 diesel and EA888/EA211 petrol engines have well-documented failure points that drive UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdown below as a decision tool to weigh repair costs against replacement value.

problems.group: Skoda Kodiaq Timing Chain Stretch & Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Skoda Kodiaq Timing Chain Stretch & Tensioner Failure - Why EA888 & EA211 Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Skoda Kodiaq 1.4 TSI, 1.5 TSI, 2.0 TSI (CZDA, DADA, DKZA, DKZB) (2017-2023)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner relies on oil pressure; prolonged oil service intervals or cold starts cause the chain to elongate and the tensioner to fail, leading to a skipping chain that can destroy the engine internals.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip front cover, replace chain, tensioner, and guides.
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 60k+ miles; only viable if caught before chain jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EA888/EA211 with upgraded tensioner.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on a cold start, act immediately. A preventative chain kit is cost-effective if the engine hasn't jumped timing. Once the chain skips, internal valve and piston damage is inevitable, making a rebuilt engine the only safe option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EA888 engine prices for your Skoda Kodiaq

problems.group: Skoda Kodiaq EGR Cooler & DPF Failure
problems.h4: Skoda Kodiaq EGR Cooler & DPF Failure - The Diesel Owner's Dilemma
problems.affectedModels: Skoda Kodiaq 2.0 TDI (DFGA, DFHA, CRVC, CRGB) (2017-2023)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler develops internal micro-cracks, leaking coolant into the intake. Combined with frequent short trips, this accelerates DPF soot and ash clogging, triggering limp mode and potential engine hydro-lock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, chemically clean DPF, reset adaptations.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if DPF substrate is intact; buys 30k-50k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.0 TDI with revised EGR calibration.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix for coolant ingestion; 12-month warranty.

problems.recommendation: If your Kodiaq is losing coolant without visible leaks and emitting white smoke, the EGR cooler has failed. If caught early, replacing the cooler and cleaning the DPF saves money. If the engine has ingested significant coolant, the bearings are compromised-replace the engine.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DFGA 2.0 TDI replacement engines

problems.group: Skoda Kodiaq Turbocharger & Oil Consumption Issues
problems.h4: Skoda Kodiaq Turbocharger & Oil Consumption Issues
problems.affectedModels: Skoda Kodiaq 2.0 TSI, 1.5 TSI (DADA, DKZA, DKZB) (2018-2023)
problems.rootCause: PCV system failures and piston ring carbon coking lead to excessive oil consumption. The resulting oil starvation damages the turbocharger bearings and scores the cylinder walls.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo, PCV valve, and clean intake tract.
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if cylinder bores are unworn.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with revised piston rings.
repairOptions.longevity: Corrects oil burn and restores compression.

problems.recommendation: Monitor your oil level weekly. If your Kodiaq is using more than 1 litre every 1,000 miles, a compression test is mandatory. If the bores are scored, a rebuilt engine with updated piston ring specifications is the only lasting solution.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned Skoda Kodiaq engine with 12-month warranty

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Skoda Kodiaq
finalCta.paragraph: Whether it's a timing chain rattle on your 2.0 TSI or a clogged DPF on your 2.0 TDI, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Skoda Kodiaq specialists. All our rebuilt engines feature upgraded components to address these exact failure points, backed by a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Skoda Kodiaq Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Skoda Kodiaq and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through the UK network with typical price ranges based on your Kodiaq variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Fully stripped, cleaned, and rebuilt to OEM specs with new wear parts. Ideal for high-mileage Kodiaqs needing reliable, long-term performance.
types.frontDescription: Fully stripped, cleaned, and rebuilt to OEM specs with new wear parts. Ideal for high-mileage Kodiaqs needing reliable, long-term performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Includes 12-month warranty. Requires professional installation and ECU adaptation.
types.backDescription: Reconditioning involves a complete teardown of your DFGA or DADA unit. All internal components are inspected, machined, and replaced with new seals, bearings, and timing chains to factory tolerances.
backBullets: Full strip-down and chemical cleaning of engine blocks
New pistons, rings, and upgraded timing chain kits fitted
Dyno-tested before dispatch to ensure factory power output
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Skoda Kodiaq engine prices

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Partially stripped with replacement of known failed components. A cost-effective solution when the bottom end is confirmed to be in good condition.
types.frontDescription: Partially stripped with replacement of known failed components. A cost-effective solution when the bottom end is confirmed to be in good condition.
types.frontDisclaimer: Warranty varies by supplier. Best for localized failures like turbo or head gasket issues.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines focus on repairing the specific failure point rather than a full overhaul. If your DKZA suffered a water pump leak that warped the head, the rebuilt unit will feature a machined cylinder head and new gaskets.
backBullets: Targeted repair of documented failure points
Cylinder head skimming and pressure testing included
Cost-effective if engine block and bores are undamaged
types.cta: Get rebuilt Skoda Kodiaq engine quotes

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage units removed from written-off or damaged Kodiaqs. The fastest, most budget-friendly route to get your SUV back on the road.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage units removed from written-off or damaged Kodiaqs. The fastest, most budget-friendly route to get your SUV back on the road.
types.frontDisclaimer: Sold as seen with limited warranty. Compression test video highly recommended before purchase.
types.backDescription: Used engines are pulled from salvage yards and tested before dispatch. They retain their original internal wear, making them a gamble for high-mileage vehicles but a smart choice for keeping costs down on older SE trims.
backBullets: Sourced from UK salvage networks with verified mileage
Compressed air and leak-down tests performed pre-sale
Ideal for budget-conscious repairs on pre-facelift models
types.cta: Check used Skoda Kodiaq engine availability

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Returned to exact blue-print specifications using new or re-machined parts. The closest you can get to a brand-new crate engine from the factory.
types.frontDescription: Returned to exact blue-print specifications using new or re-machined parts. The closest you can get to a brand-new crate engine from the factory.
types.frontDisclaimer: Premium pricing reflects OEM-grade machining. Lead times may apply for rare vRS codes.
types.backDescription: Remanufacturing is the gold standard. Every component is measured against original Skoda engineering specs. Blocks are line-bored, crankshafts are reground, and all electronics are refreshed.
backBullets: Machined to original factory blueprint tolerances
New OEM gaskets, seals, and fasteners used throughout
Best option for late-model Sportline and L&K variants
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Skoda Kodiaq engine costs

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Refurbished Engines
types.description: Externally cleaned and fitted with new peripheral components like sensors and gaskets. A middle-ground option for minor leak or seal failures.
types.frontDescription: Externally cleaned and fitted with new peripheral components like sensors and gaskets. A middle-ground option for minor leak or seal failures.
types.frontDisclaimer: Does not involve internal engine machining. Suitable for external seal and accessory failures.
types.backDescription: Refurbishment addresses the outer layers of the engine. If your Kodiaq is suffering from oil leaks or faulty sensors, a refurbished unit saves money by reusing the verified internal rotating assembly.
backBullets: New valve cover gaskets, seals, and sensors fitted
External carbon buildup cleaned from intake manifold
Cost-effective for resolving peripheral engine issues
types.cta: View refurbished Skoda Kodiaq engine options

types.title: Skoda Kodiaq Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: The hassle-free option. Your chosen engine is delivered and installed by a vetted local specialist, complete with tracking and ECU coding.
types.frontDescription: The hassle-free option. Your chosen engine is delivered and installed by a vetted local specialist, complete with tracking and ECU coding.
types.frontDisclaimer: Labour costs vary by region and variant complexity (e.g., 4x4 Haldex removal).
types.backDescription: Supply and fit bundles remove the stress of finding a reliable garage. The specialist handles the heavy lifting, DSG adaptation, and AdBlue calibration, ensuring your Kodiaq is ready to drive away with zero downtime.
backBullets: Vetted UK garages with Kodiaq-specific diagnostic tools
Includes DSG mechatronic adaptation and ECU coding
Single point of contact for engine and labour warranty
types.cta: Find Skoda Kodiaq engine supply & fit deals

engineSizes.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Skoda Kodiaq is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, and common failure notes to help you identify the exact unit.

items.title: Skoda Kodiaq 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre TDI (EA288) is the undisputed workhorse of the Kodiaq range, powering everything from entry-level SE models to the rugged Scout and high-output vRS. Known for massive low-end torque, it is the preferred choice for towing and motorway cruising, though emissions systems require attention on city-driven examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Skoda Kodiaq 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Skoda Kodiaq 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TSI (EA211) served as the entry-level petrol option during the early pre-facelift years. It offers adequate performance for lighter 5-seat configurations and avoids the complexity of diesel emissions systems, making it a budget-friendly choice for urban SUV buyers.
commonFailurePoints: carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves.
items.cta: Compare Skoda Kodiaq 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Kodiaq 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Replacing the 1.4 in 2018, the 1.5 TSI Evo introduced cylinder deactivation (ACT) and improved thermal efficiency. It became the standard petrol choice for mid-range trims, balancing fuel economy with sufficient power for the heavy Kodiaq body.
commonFailurePoints: Early EA211 evo timing chain tensioner issues
items.cta: Compare Skoda Kodiaq 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Kodiaq 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: A rare early-production petrol unit, the 1.8 TSI was quickly phased out in favour of the more robust 2.0 TSI architecture. It provided a middle ground for buyers wanting petrol refinement before the EA888 Gen3B became widely available.
items.cta: Compare Skoda Kodiaq 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Kodiaq 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The EA888 Gen3 and Gen3B 2.0 TSI engines deliver strong performance, powering the Sportline, L&K, and the flagship vRS. With robust internals, they are highly tunable but require strict adherence to oil service intervals to prevent timing chain and water pump failures.
items.cta: Compare Skoda Kodiaq 2.0L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Skoda Kodiaq needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Skoda Kodiaq specialists.

items.descriptor: Introduced in 2022, the 1.5 TSI e-TEC mild hybrid system uses a 48-volt battery to assist acceleration and enable sailing, improving urban fuel economy.
families: Electric torque fills turbo lag during initial acceleration
Engine replacement requires specialist 48V system isolation and testing

engineYears.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Skoda Kodiaq's production history, helping you quickly identify your engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Kodiaq arrives on the MQB platform, introducing the EA288 diesel and early EA211/EA888 petrol engines to the UK SUV market.
keyChanges: Skoda Kodiaq launches in the UK with 5 and 7-seat configurations
Introduction of 2.0 TDI (DFGA/CRVA) and 1.4 TSI (CZDA) engines
4x4 Haldex system available on higher trims
knownFor: Early 1.4 TSI units prone to timing chain stretch
Solid build quality but infotainment software lagged behind rivals
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2018 Skoda Kodiaq replacement engines
years.ticker: Skoda Kodiaq 2017 engine replacement • Kodiaq 1.4 TSI timing chain • Kodiaq 2.0 TDI DFGA cost

years.description: The 1.4 TSI is replaced by the efficient 1.5 TSI Evo, while diesel engines update to meet strict Euro 6d-temp emissions standards.
keyChanges: 1.5 TSI (DADA/DPCA) replaces 1.4 TSI with ACT cylinder deactivation
Diesel engines update to DTSB/CRGB codes for Euro 6d compliance
AdBlue SCR systems become standard on all diesel variants
knownFor: 1.5 TSI offers significantly better fuel economy than 1.4
AdBlue heater failures common on early Euro 6d diesels
Peak production years with highest stock availability
years.cta: Compare prices for 2019-2020 Skoda Kodiaq engines
years.ticker: Kodiaq 1.5 TSI DADA replacement • Kodiaq AdBlue fault • Kodiaq 2019 engine cost

years.description: The facelift brings updated styling, revised ECU calibrations, and the introduction of the 48V mild hybrid system for the 1.5 TSI.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift with new LED matrix headlights
Introduction of 1.5 TSI e-TEC mild hybrid (DXDB)
Final production years of MK1 before PS7 chassis launch
knownFor: vRS models highly sought after for performance SUV segment
Mild hybrid requires specialist 48V testing during engine swaps
Excellent reliability on late-model EA888 Gen3B units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-2023 Skoda Kodiaq replacement engines
years.ticker: Kodiaq vRS engine replacement • Kodiaq 1.5 mHEV DXDB • Kodiaq facelift engine compatibility

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Skoda Kodiaq was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Skoda Kodiaq specialists.

faq.h2: Skoda Kodiaq Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Skoda Kodiaq engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Skoda Kodiaq engine replacement typically costs between £1,800 and £3,500 for a rebuilt unit, depending on the engine code and variant. Supply and fit services usually add £600 to £900 in labour costs.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £1,200 for early 1.4 TSI or 2.0 TDI units.
Reconditioned 2.0 TDI (DFGA) engines average £1,800 to £2,600.
Performance vRS engines (DKZB/CUAA) can exceed £3,500 due to rarity.
Supply and fit bundles include DSG adaptation and ECU coding.
items.cta: Get an instant Skoda Kodiaq engine replacement quote

items.question: What are the most common Skoda Kodiaq engine problems?
items.answer: The most frequent issues include EGR cooler leaks on 2.0 TDI diesels and timing chain stretch on 1.5 TSI and 2.0 TSI petrol engines. These failures often trigger the engine management light and limp mode.
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI (EA288) suffers from DPF clogging and EGR coolant leaks.
1.5 TSI (EA211) and 2.0 TSI (EA888) experience timing chain tensioner wear.
Ignoring a rattling cold start can lead to catastrophic internal engine damage.
Rebuilt units from EngineMarket include upgraded tensioners and revised EGR coolers.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine prices for your Skoda Kodiaq

items.question: How do I verify my Skoda Kodiaq engine code compatibility?
items.answer: You can verify your engine code by entering your registration number into our lookup tool or checking the sticker in the service book and under the bonnet. Matching the exact code (e.g., DFGA, DKZA) is critical for ECU and DSG compatibility.
keyPoints: Engine codes are 3- or 4-letter codes found on the timing belt cover or ECU sticker.
Fitting the wrong code will cause DSG mechatronic and immobilizer errors.
Our free DVLA lookup tool instantly matches your VIN to the correct engine code.
Never guess the code; cross-reference with your V5C logbook or specialist.
items.cta: Check Skoda Kodiaq engine compatibility free

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Skoda Kodiaq engine?
items.answer: If the repair cost exceeds 60% of a rebuilt engine's price, replacement is almost always the better financial choice. Rebuilt engines come with a 12-month warranty and upgraded components, offering better long-term value than patching old failures.
keyPoints: Timing chain repairs on high-mileage EA888 engines often reveal further wear.
A rebuilt engine resets the clock on internal components like bearings and rings.
Main dealer repairs are often uneconomical on 5-year-old Kodiaq models.
EngineMarket specialists provide transparent repair vs. replace advice.
items.cta: Find out if your Skoda Kodiaq needs a replacement engine

items.question: Does a reconditioned Skoda Kodiaq engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned or rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Some specialists offer extended 24-month or 36-month options for added peace of mind.
keyPoints: The warranty covers all internal mechanical components and oil seals.
It is valid UK-wide and transferable if you sell the vehicle within the period.
Proper installation by a vetted specialist and correct servicing are required.
You can upgrade to a 36-month warranty at checkout for a small fee.
items.cta: View Skoda Kodiaq engine warranty options

items.question: How long does a Skoda Kodiaq engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: A typical supply and fit service takes 1 to 3 days. If the engine is in stock at a local specialist, the Kodiaq can often be dropped off in the morning and collected by late afternoon the next day.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery is available for popular codes like DFGA and DADA.
Complex 4x4 or vRS models may require an extra day for Haldex or ECU coding.
The process includes draining fluids, swapping the unit, and resetting adaptations.
You will receive a live tracking link once your engine is dispatched.
items.cta: Book Skoda Kodiaq engine supply and fit today

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Skoda Kodiaq MOT or emissions?
items.answer: No, as long as the replacement engine matches the original specifications and emissions standard (e.g., Euro 6). Rebuilt engines are tested to ensure they pass MOT emissions limits for diesel particulates and NOx.
keyPoints: Replacing a like-for-like engine code does not require DVLA notification.
AdBlue and DPF systems must be fully functional to pass the MOT test.
EngineMarket suppliers verify emissions compliance before dispatch.
Never opt for "DPF deleted" engines, as they will fail the MOT instantly.
items.cta: Ensure your Skoda Kodiaq replacement is MOT compliant

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Kodiaq engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are fully stripped down to the bare block and machined to OEM specs with all new wear parts. Rebuilt engines are partially stripped, focusing only on replacing the specific components that failed.
keyPoints: Reconditioned is the premium option, ideal for high-mileage or vRS models.
Rebuilt is more cost-effective if the engine block and bores are undamaged.
Both options from EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month warranty.
Our specialists will recommend the best condition type based on your Kodiaq's history.
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Skoda Kodiaq engines

items.question: Can I fit a used Skoda Kodiaq engine myself to save money?
items.answer: While physically possible, we strongly advise against DIY fitting. Modern Kodiaqs require specialized diagnostic tools for ECU coding, DSG adaptation, and AdBlue resets, which most home mechanics do not possess.
keyPoints: Incorrect ECU coding will immobilize the vehicle and corrupt the immobilizer.
DSG mechatronic units require precise calibration to prevent transmission damage.
DIY fitting voids the engine warranty provided by the supplier.
Using our supply and fit network ensures professional installation and full warranty coverage.
items.cta: Find a specialist for Skoda Kodiaq engine fitting

items.question: How do I get a quote for my Skoda Kodiaq engine replacement?
items.answer: Simply enter your registration number or engine code into the tool at the top of this page. Within seconds, you will receive multiple tailored quotes from vetted UK engine specialists, with no obligation to book.
keyPoints: The lookup tool instantly identifies your exact variant and engine code.
Quotes include options for used, reconditioned, and supply & fit services.
You can compare prices, warranty terms, and turnaround times side-by-side.
There is no pressure to buy; the service is 100% free and secure.
items.cta: Get your free Skoda Kodiaq engine quote now
